description: Places a point on a 2D graph using Cartesian coordinates. Accepts separate x,y values or a point tuple expression. For polar coordinates use polarpoint(), for ratio along shapes use ratiopoint(), for angle on circles use anglepoint(), for points on plots use plotpoint().
utility: Place a point at Cartesian coordinates on a graph

arguments.name: x | tuple
arguments.description: X coordinate, or a point tuple expression like (3, 4) or f(t)

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Y coordinate (omit when passing a tuple)

arguments.name: icon
arguments.description: Point marker icon style. dot (default filled circle), cross (x), open (hollow circle), plus (+), square

arguments.name: styles
arguments.type: c() | fc() | s() | defer() | notes() | icon()
arguments.description: Color, fill, size (s() controls point radius), visibility, notes, or marker icon

alternates.description: Point from an inline point tuple
Point from a function that returns a point tuple
Point from a matrix multiplied with a point tuple
Marker icons: icon(dot) (default), icon(cross), icon(open), icon(plus), icon(square)
Creates a point for reference but defers rendering

examples.scenario: Point at center of a circle with size
